question_text: What is the key advantage of using a parallel circuit for domestic electrical installations?

options.label: A
options.text: It uses less wire than a series circuit

options.label: B
options.text: Each appliance can operate independently and receive full voltage

options.label: C
options.text: It prevents all electrical devices from working if one fails

options.label: D
options.text: It requires less current from the power supply

correct_answer: Each appliance can operate independently and receive full voltage
explanation: Parallel circuits are used in homes because each appliance is connected separately to the power supply. This means each device receives the full voltage (230V in the UK) and can be switched on or off without affecting other appliances. If one device breaks, the others continue to work normally.
